description: Phase 1: Django+React internal platform — PWA audit tool, sync to backend, R2 media, audit review with streaming Claude agent, project landing + versioning.

userStories.title: Enable pgvector extension in Postgres
userStories.description: As a developer, I need pgvector enabled so KnowledgeDoc embeddings work later.
acceptanceCriteria: Postgres image in docker-compose.local.yml swapped to pgvector/pgvector:pg16 (or extension installed in existing image)
Django migration runs `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector`
`pgvector` Python package added to pyproject.toml
Backend tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire Django Channels with Redis channel layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need ASGI + Channels configured so streaming WebSocket consumers work.
acceptanceCriteria: `channels` and `channels-redis` added to pyproject.toml
`config/asgi.py` wired with ProtocolTypeRouter + AuthMiddlewareStack + URLRouter
`config/routing.py` created with empty websocket_urlpatterns list
Redis channel layer configured in settings via CHANNEL_LAYERS
Server starts via daphne/uvicorn without errors
Backend tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Cloudflare R2 storage settings (no usage yet)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need R2 env vars + django-storages S3 backend configured for later media wiring.
acceptanceCriteria: `django-storages[s3]` and `boto3` added to pyproject.toml
R2 settings in config/settings/base.py: R2_ACCOUNT_ID, R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID, R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, R2_BUCKET, R2_ENDPOINT_URL, R2_PUBLIC_URL
.envs/.local/.django updated with placeholder R2 vars
S3Boto3Storage subclass for R2 created with endpoint_url override
Backend tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Install frontend deps (TanStack, Vitest, Tailwind)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TanStack Query/Table/Router, Vitest + Testing Library, Tailwind installed in frontend/.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/package.json adds: @tanstack/react-query, @tanstack/react-table, @tanstack/react-router, axios
Adds: vitest, @testing-library/react, @testing-library/jest-dom, jsdom
Adds: tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer; tailwind.config + postcss.config + base CSS created
vitest.config.ts created with jsdom env + setup file
Starter test in src/__tests__ passes via `npm run test`
`npm run build` succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Configure Vite proxy + frontend API client
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Vite dev server to proxy /api and /ws to Django, and a typed fetch client.
acceptanceCriteria: vite.config.ts proxies /api → http://localhost:8001 and /ws → ws://localhost:8001
src/lib/api.ts: axios instance with baseURL=/api, CSRF + auth handling
src/lib/ws.ts: WebSocket helper that connects to /ws/<path>
TanStack QueryClient provider mounted in src/main.tsx
Frontend tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add justfile recipes for frontend + combined test
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unified just commands for frontend dev/build/test and a combined test runner.
acceptanceCriteria: justfile adds: `frontend-dev`, `frontend-build`, `frontend-test`, `frontend-lint`
justfile adds: `test` recipe that runs backend pytest + frontend vitest
Each recipe runs successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CI workflow runs backend + frontend tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need GitHub Actions to gate merges on both test suites.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/ci.yml runs on PR + push to main
Job 1: backend pytest + ruff lint (Postgres + Redis services in CI)
Job 2: frontend `npm ci && npm run lint && npm run test && npm run build`
Both jobs required for merge
CI passes on current main

userStories.title: Add Project model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Project table to group buildings.
acceptanceCriteria: Project model in light_audit/audit/models.py: name, client, building_type, status, created_at, updated_at, owner FK to User
Migration generated + applied cleanly
Admin registration
Pytest covers basic CRUD
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Link Building → Project, migrate client field
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Building.project FK and existing Building.client value migrated onto Project.
acceptanceCriteria: Building gains project = FK(Project, related_name='buildings', null=True initially)
Data migration creates Project from existing Building.client values (or assigns to a default Project)
After data migration, Building.project made non-null
Building.client field removed
Pytest covers migration outcome on fixture data
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add AuditVersion model + snapshot FKs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need AuditVersion to snapshot field captures and link rooms/floors/log entries to a version.
acceptanceCriteria: Auto-increment version_number in AuditVersion model on save (per building)
Pytest covers version_number sequencing + cascade
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enforce published-version immutability
userStories.description: As a developer, I need published AuditVersion rows to reject edits to themselves + descendants.
acceptanceCriteria: AuditVersion.clean() raises if status='published' and existing instance modified
Floor/Room/LogEntry.save() rejects edits if their audit_version.status='published'
Admin guards published versions
Pytest covers reject + allow paths
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add KnowledgeDoc model with pgvector column
userStories.description: As a developer, I need schema placeholder for embeddings (no ingest).
acceptanceCriteria: KnowledgeDoc model: title, source_path, chunk_text, embedding VectorField(dimensions=1536) via pgvector.django
Migration applies cleanly against pgvector-enabled Postgres
Pytest creates + retrieves a row (without semantic search)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Photo model + signals
userStories.description: As a developer, I need media metadata table linked to log entries.
acceptanceCriteria: Photo model fields: building FK, floor FK (null), room FK (null), space_name, photo_type ('fixture'|'switch'|'controls'|'panorama'|'video'), storage_path, public_url, thumbnail_url, file_size_bytes, mime_type, width, height, duration_seconds, taken_at, uploaded_at, user FK, log_entry FK (null), notes
Migration applied
Admin registration
Pytest covers FK relations
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add AuditFlag model with dismissal
userStories.description: As a developer, I need dismissible flag rows attached to log entries.
acceptanceCriteria: AuditFlag: log_entry FK, audit_version FK, severity ('info'|'warn'|'critical'), message, status ('active'|'dismissed'), dismissed_reason, dismissed_by FK User (null), dismissed_at, source_run_id (null FK to AgentRun)
Migration applied
Pytest covers active/dismissed transitions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add AgentRun model for Claude call logging
userStories.description: As a developer, I need every Claude API call recorded.
acceptanceCriteria: Pytest covers basic create + status transitions for AgentRun model
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Auth API: login/logout endpoints
userStories.description: As an internal user, I want to log in via API.
acceptanceCriteria: Django ninja endpoints: POST /api/auth/login, POST /api/auth/logout, GET /api/auth/me
Session auth (cookie-based) with CSRF
Email used as USERNAME_FIELD on custom User model (or auth backend that accepts email)
All non-auth API endpoints require authentication (django ninja default permission)
Pytest covers happy path + 401 on unauth
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend: login + password reset pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want React pages for login, reset request, reset confirm.
acceptanceCriteria: Routes: /login, /password-reset, /password-reset/confirm/:token
Forms use TanStack Query mutations against /api/auth/*
Redirect to / on successful login; logged-in users redirected away from /login
Auth context/provider tracks current user
Frontend tests cover form submission + auth context
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Django ninja schemas + api endpoints for Project/Building/AuditVersion
userStories.description: As a developer, I need REST endpoints to list/retrieve projects, buildings, versions.
acceptanceCriteria: django ninja endpoints registered at /api/projects, /api/buildings, /api/audit-versions
Nested routes: /api/projects/{id}/buildings, /api/buildings/{id}/audit-versions
Read-only for now (no PUT/PATCH); POST for create
Auth required
Pytest covers list + retrieve + permissions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Sync API: POST /api/audits/sync
userStories.description: As a PWA client, I want to push a full audit payload that creates an AuditVersion snapshot.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/audits/sync accepts JSON: { building_uuid, base_version_uuid (null), payload: { rooms[], log_entries[], ... } }
Creates new AuditVersion + child Floor/Room/LogEntry records
Idempotent on client-supplied UUIDs (replays return existing version)
Stores raw payload on AuditVersion.source_payload
Pytest covers create + idempotent replay
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Sync API: GET version pull + push-to-iPad marker
userStories.description: As a PWA client, I want to pull a specific version and see which is available to load.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/audits/{version_id} returns full payload for PWA hydration
GET /api/projects/{id}/audits lists versions
POST /api/audit-versions/{id}/push-to-ipad sets status='pushed_to_ipad'
GET /api/buildings/{id}/available-version returns version with pushed_to_ipad status if newer than client
Pytest covers each endpoint
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: R2 media: presigned multipart upload endpoints
userStories.description: As a PWA client, I want to upload large files directly to R2 via S3 multipart.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/media/multipart/start → creates Photo row (status=uploading), calls R2 CreateMultipartUpload, returns upload_id + storage_path
POST /api/media/multipart/sign-part → returns presigned PUT URL for a given part_number
POST /api/media/multipart/complete → calls R2 CompleteMultipartUpload with part ETags, updates Photo (status=uploaded, public_url)
POST /api/media/multipart/abort → calls AbortMultipartUpload
Pytest covers each endpoint with mocked boto3
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Celery task: generate thumbnail on Photo upload
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a 300x300 thumbnail produced after upload.
acceptanceCriteria: Celery task `generate_thumbnail(photo_id)` downloads from R2, resizes via Pillow, uploads thumbnail to R2 path
Task triggered on Photo.status transition to 'uploaded' (post_save signal)
Photo.thumbnail_url populated on completion
Pytest covers task with mocked R2 + Pillow
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: html_app bundler script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a bundler that inlines split files OR passes through single-file html_app.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/bundle_html_app.py (or .js) reads html_app/app.html
If app.html has placeholder/link refs, inlines app.css into <style> and app.js into <script>
If app.html already has inline <style>/<script>, passes through unchanged
Emits dist/audit-pwa/index.html
justfile recipe: `just html-app-build`
Tests cover both split-file and single-file inputs
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: PWA manifest + iOS meta + icons
userStories.description: As a field auditor, I want the audit tool installable on iPad.
acceptanceCriteria: html_app/manifest.json with name, icons (192, 512), start_url, display: standalone, theme/background color
iOS meta tags injected into app.html (apple-mobile-web-app-capable, status-bar-style, apple-touch-icon links)
App icons placed in html_app/icons/
Bundler copies manifest + icons to dist/audit-pwa/
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: PWA service worker with versioned cache
userStories.description: As a field auditor, I want the PWA to boot fully offline.
acceptanceCriteria: html_app/sw.js implements cache-first strategy with version-stamped cache key
Caches index.html, manifest, icons, and any external assets
Service worker registration script added to app.html
Old cache versions cleaned on activate
Lighthouse PWA audit passes installable criteria
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Django route serves bundled PWA at /audit/
userStories.description: As a field auditor, I want to load the PWA from the Django app.
acceptanceCriteria: Django view serves dist/audit-pwa/index.html at /audit/
Static assets (sw.js, manifest.json, icons/) served with correct MIME types
Service-Worker-Allowed header set; sw.js served with no-cache
Pytest covers route returns 200 + correct content-type
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: PWA IndexedDB storage module (new file)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a self-contained IndexedDB module for blobs + audit JSON that html_app/app.js can call.
acceptanceCriteria: html_app/storage.js exposes: getJSON(key), setJSON(key, value), putBlob(id, blob, meta), getBlob(id), listBlobs(filter), deleteBlob(id)
Uses idb wrapper or raw IndexedDB; two object stores: 'json' and 'blobs'
Bundler inlines storage.js after app.js
Frontend tests (vitest + fake-indexeddb) cover read/write/delete
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: PWA localStorage shim + auto-migrate to IndexedDB
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a localStorage shim that proxies to IndexedDB so app.js JSON storage just works, and any existing localStorage data migrates on first boot.
acceptanceCriteria: html_app/storage-shim.js installs window.__auditStorage proxy with in-memory cache hydrated from IndexedDB on boot
Provides sync getItem/setItem/removeItem API
On first boot, copies all existing localStorage keys to IndexedDB then clears localStorage
Boot blocks app.js execution until hydration complete
Frontend tests cover shim + migration
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: PWA media capture: store Blobs in IndexedDB
userStories.description: As a field auditor, I want photo/video captures stored as Blobs (not base64) in IndexedDB.
acceptanceCriteria: Edit app.js photo/video capture call sites to call storage.putBlob() instead of base64-into-JSON
Audit JSON references blobs by id, not inline data
Existing base64 entries auto-migrated to blob store on first boot
Frontend tests cover capture + retrieval
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: PWA client-side photo compression
userStories.description: As a field auditor, I want photos resized to ~2MB before storage.
acceptanceCriteria: html_app/compress.js: resizes via canvas, JPEG quality tuned to ~2MB target
EXIF timestamp extracted via exif-js (or raw parse) and preserved
Capture pipeline runs compress before storage.putBlob()
Frontend tests cover compression output size + EXIF retention
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: PWA video length cap
userStories.description: As a field auditor, I want videos over 30s rejected with a message.
acceptanceCriteria: Video capture handler reads duration; rejects > 30s
User-facing message rendered on rejection
Panoramas (photo_type='panorama') exempt from compression
Frontend tests cover accept + reject paths
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: video-cap.js: checkVideoDuration + captureWithPolicy; cleanup nulls handlers before src='' to prevent infinite loop in tests; rejects non-image/non-video files.

userStories.title: PWA sync queue: enqueue changes offline
userStories.description: As a field auditor, I want audit data + media uploads queued when offline.
acceptanceCriteria: html_app/sync-queue.js: persists queue items in IndexedDB ('sync_queue' store)
Queue items: { id, type ('audit'|'media'), payload | blob_id, retry_count, last_error }
Public API: enqueueAudit(payload), enqueueMedia(blob_id, meta), peek(), markDone(id), markFailed(id, err)
Frontend tests cover enqueue, retry counter, drain order
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: sync-queue.js opens auditDB at v2 (bumped from storage.js v1) so onupgradeneeded adds sync_queue store alongside existing json/blobs stores. Uses autoIncrement for drain order.

userStories.title: PWA sync drain: in-app polling loop
userStories.description: As a field auditor, I want queued items uploaded automatically while PWA is open and connectivity returns.
acceptanceCriteria: Polling loop runs every 10s while online + PWA visible
Audit items POST to /api/audits/sync
Media items use R2 multipart endpoints (start → sign-part → complete)
Exponential backoff on failure (10s → 30s → 2m → 10m)
UI status indicator shows queue length + active uploads
Frontend tests cover drain + backoff
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: sync-drain.js: polling via setTimeout chain; _tick checks online+visible before draining. Backoff index resets on clean pass. CustomEvent 'sync-status' emitted for UI badge. Bundler refactored to _inline_module helper for all ES modules.

userStories.title: Frontend: projects list page
userStories.description: As an internal user, I want to see all projects.
acceptanceCriteria: Route /projects renders list via TanStack Query against /api/projects
Each row: name, client, status, building count
Click row → /projects/{id}
Frontend tests cover list render + navigation
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added building_count to ProjectSchema via resolve_building_count + Count annotation. ProjectsListPage uses useQuery + useNavigate. 4 frontend tests cover render, navigation, empty state, error state.

userStories.title: Frontend: project detail with buildings + versions
userStories.description: As an internal user, I want to see a project's buildings and each building's audit versions.
acceptanceCriteria: Route /projects/{id} shows project metadata + buildings list
Each building expands to TanStack Table of audit versions (number, label, status, created_by, created_at)
Frontend tests cover render + table sort
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added created_by_name to AuditVersionSchema. ProjectDetailPage uses expandable buildings with BuildingVersionsTable sub-component using TanStack Table with sorting. 5 frontend tests cover render, expand, sort, error, empty states.

userStories.title: Frontend: push-to-iPad + duplicate version actions
userStories.description: As an internal user, I want buttons to push a version to iPad and to duplicate a version.
acceptanceCriteria: Push button calls POST /api/audit-versions/{id}/push-to-ipad; row status updates
Duplicate button creates new draft version from existing one (backend endpoint POST /api/audit-versions/{id}/duplicate added)
Pytest covers duplicate endpoint
Frontend tests cover button actions
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Backend: POST /api/audit-versions/{id}/duplicate/ creates draft copy with copied source_payload + label='Copy of X'. Frontend: ActionButtons component with push-to-ipad + duplicate useMutation calls; invalidates building-versions query on success. 6 backend tests + 2 new frontend button tests.

userStories.title: Frontend: building → floor → room navigation
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want to navigate into a specific room within an audit version.
acceptanceCriteria: Routes: /audit-versions/{id}, /audit-versions/{id}/floors/{floor_id}, /audit-versions/{id}/rooms/{room_id}
Sidebar tree of floors → rooms; click navigates
Frontend tests cover tree render + navigation
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: FloorTreeSidebar shared component fetches floors+rooms; 3 pages (AuditVersionPage, AuditVersionFloorPage, AuditVersionRoomPage); backend adds /audit-versions/{id}/floors/ and /audit-versions/{id}/rooms/{room_id}/ endpoints. 7 frontend tests + tsc clean.

userStories.title: Frontend: room view with log entries table (read-only)
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want to see all log entries for a room.
acceptanceCriteria: Room view renders log entries via TanStack Table
Columns: fixture_id, qty, wattage, switch_type, controls, mount_type, flags (icons), notes
Read-only; sortable + filterable
Frontend tests cover render + sort
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Backend: GET /audit-versions/{id}/rooms/{room_id}/log-entries/ + LogEntrySchema. Frontend: LogEntriesTable component with TanStack Table (sort by fixture_id/qty/wattage/switch/controls/mount; global filter input; flag boolean columns rendered as colored badges). 5 frontend tests: render, flags, values, sort, filter, empty state.

userStories.title: Frontend: room photo grid + lightbox
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want to see photos for a room and open full-resolution on click.
acceptanceCriteria: Photo grid renders thumbnails (thumbnail_url) for room's photos
Click thumbnail opens lightbox with full-resolution public_url
Keyboard nav (arrow keys + Esc)
Frontend tests cover grid + lightbox open/close
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Backend: GET /audit-versions/{id}/rooms/{room_id}/photos/ + PhotoSchema (filters upload_status=uploaded). Frontend: PhotoGrid component with thumbnail grid + Lightbox modal (Esc/arrow key nav, prev/next buttons, click backdrop to close). 5 frontend tests + 3 backend tests.

userStories.title: Frontend: panorama viewer (Pannellum)
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want 360° panoramas to open in Pannellum, not as flat images.
acceptanceCriteria: Pannellum installed; PanoramaViewer component wraps it
Photos with photo_type='panorama' open in PanoramaViewer instead of standard lightbox
Frontend tests cover dispatch (unit-test the type check + component choice)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend: render AuditFlags inline + dismiss UI
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want to see flags on log entry rows and dismiss them with a reason.
acceptanceCriteria: Flag column in log entries table shows severity-colored badges
Click badge opens flag detail panel with message + dismiss button
Dismiss opens modal asking for reason → POST /api/audit-flags/{id}/dismiss { reason } (backend endpoint added)
Pytest covers dismiss endpoint
Frontend tests cover badge + dismiss modal flow

userStories.title: Anthropic SDK wrapper that enforces AgentRun logging
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a single wrapper that every Claude call goes through and that auto-creates AgentRun rows.
acceptanceCriteria: audit/llm.py exposes `run_agent(agent_type, user, project, audit_version, messages, stream=False)` context manager
Creates AgentRun on enter with status='running'; updates on exit with response, tokens, status
Records errors with stack trace
Direct calls to anthropic SDK outside wrapper prohibited (lint rule or test)
Pytest mocks SDK + asserts AgentRun created
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Audit review Channels consumer (streaming)
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want chat messages to stream from Claude over WebSocket.
acceptanceCriteria: AsyncJsonWebsocketConsumer at /ws/audit-review/{audit_version_id}/
Auth-gated (rejects anonymous)
Receives { prompt, predefined_prompt_id (null) }; calls llm.run_agent with audit data context, streams tokens back
Conversation history maintained per consumer connection
Pytest covers consumer using channels.testing.WebsocketCommunicator
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Predefined prompts seed + admin
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a place to manage predefined audit-review prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: PredefinedPrompt model: name, prompt_text, agent_type, active bool
Migration + admin registration
Seed fixture with 2-3 placeholder prompts (content TBD)
GET /api/predefined-prompts endpoint
Pytest covers list endpoint
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend: audit review chat panel
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want a chat panel on the audit review page with predefined prompt dropdown + freeform input + streaming output.
acceptanceCriteria: Chat panel mounted on /audit-versions/{id} view
Predefined prompt dropdown populated from /api/predefined-prompts
Send opens WebSocket to /ws/audit-review/{id}/ and streams tokens into message bubble
Iterative follow-ups append to same conversation

userStories.title: Persist flags from agent run + inject dismissed reasons

userStories.title: Audit spreadsheet export (CSV/XLSX)

userStories.title: html_app data-sync alignment review doc
userStories.description: As a stakeholder, I need a written analysis of html_app's sync payload vs backend needs.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/html-app-data-sync-review.md created
Enumerates every field emitted by html_app/app.js in sync payload
Maps each field to a Django model field (or flags as unmapped/lossy/ambiguous)
Lists fields backend needs but PWA does not emit
Lists fields PWA emits that nothing consumes
Flags Phase 2 spec-engine blockers
Recommendations for minimal PWA changes for Phase 1 end-to-end sync

userStories.title: Deployment: Django ASGI + Celery on Render (or equivalent)
userStories.description: As an internal user, I want the platform reachable at a custom domain.
acceptanceCriteria: render.yaml updated: web service runs daphne/uvicorn (ASGI), worker runs Celery, beat service
Managed Postgres with pgvector + Redis provisioned
Frontend production build served (via Django whitenoise or static host)
Custom domain + HTTPS enforced
Env vars + secrets documented in README
Deploy succeeds; smoke test: login + load /projects works in prod
CI passes

userStories.title: Auth API: password reset flow
userStories.description: As a user, I want to reset my password via email link.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/auth/password-reset → sends email with token link
POST /api/auth/password-reset/confirm → accepts token + new password
Email template renders with reset URL pointing to frontend route
Mailpit receives email in dev
Pytest covers token issue + confirm + invalid-token reject
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Uses Django PasswordResetTokenGenerator; user-enumeration safe (always 200 on request); token invalidates after password change. FRONTEND_URL setting controls reset link base URL.
